{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Manager",
  "description": "Universal Human Resource Consult is a human resource consulting firm that effectively manages private and government organization by ensuring adequate staff recruitment and selection, staff training and co-ordination thereby creating sustainable growth and increase quality of service delivery in organizations.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Human Resource Manager (Spa: Health &amp; Wellness)&nbsp;

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Human Resources Manager is responsible for developing and implementing people strategies that attract, develop, engage, and retain high-performing employees while fostering a positive workplace culture.
	This role oversees the full employee lifecycle, including workforce planning, recruitment, onboarding, learning and development, performance management, compensation and benefits, employee relations, policy implementation, compliance, and organizational development.
	Working closely with executive leadership, the HR Manager ensures that the organization&#39;s human capital strategy aligns with business objectives while promoting legal compliance, employee well-being, operational excellence, and exceptional customer service.
	Within the Spa, Beauty, Wellness, and Personal Care industry, the HR Manager also ensures staffing levels support excellent client experiences and that employees uphold the company&#39;s service standards, professionalism, and brand values.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute HR strategies that align with organizational goals.
	Design workforce plans that support business expansion.
	Advise management on organizational structure and manpower planning.
	Develop succession plans for key business positions.
	Build a strong talent pipeline for future staffing needs.
	Improve employer branding initiatives.
	Develop comprehensive onboarding programs.
	Ensure new hires understand organizational policies and culture.
	Conduct annual Training Needs Assessments (TNA).
	Develop learning and development plans.
	Drive employee engagement initiatives.
	Organize staff recognition programmes.
	Promote diversity, equity, and inclusion.


Salary
N6,000,000 annually.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs in PDF to: uhrrecruitment@outlook.com using &quot;HUMAN RESOURCES MANAGER&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Human Resource Manager Job at Universal Human Resource Consult




        
    

        
        Universal Human Resource Consult is a human resource consulting firm that effectively manages private and government organization by ensuring adequate staff recruitment and selection, staff training and co-ordination thereby creating sustainable growth and increase quality of service delivery in organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resource Manager (Spa: Health & Wellness) 

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Human Resources Manager is responsible for developing and implementing people strategies that attract, develop, engage, and retain high-performing employees while fostering a positive workplace culture.
	This role oversees the full employee lifecycle, including workforce planning, recruitment, onboarding, learning and development, performance management, compensation and benefits, employee relations, policy implementation, compliance, and organizational development.
	Working closely with executive leadership, the HR Manager ensures that the organization's human capital strategy aligns with business objectives while promoting legal compliance, employee well-being, operational excellence, and exceptional customer service.
	Within the Spa, Beauty, Wellness, and Personal Care industry, the HR Manager also ensures staffing levels support excellent client experiences and that employees uphold the company's service standards, professionalism, and brand values.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute HR strategies that align with organizational goals.
	Design workforce plans that support business expansion.
	Advise management on organizational structure and manpower planning.
	Develop succession plans for key business positions.
	Build a strong talent pipeline for future staffing needs.
	Improve employer branding initiatives.
	Develop comprehensive onboarding programs.
	Ensure new hires understand organizational policies and culture.
	Conduct annual Training Needs Assessments (TNA).
	Develop learning and development plans.
	Drive employee engagement initiatives.
	Organize staff recognition programmes.
	Promote diversity, equity, and inclusion.


Salary
N6,000,000 annually.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs in PDF to: [email protected] using "HUMAN RESOURCES MANAGER" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Manager",
  "description": "Oliver Twist Supermarket is the brainchild of enigmatic directors along with a strong leadership and advisory board. The directors&rsquo; vast knowledge and experience in retail business have been deployed in the management and operation of Oliver Twist Supermarket since the inception of the first store in 2019 in Abuja, Nigeria. Our passion for the retail industry is not just to create financial success and growth but also to build a standard of excellence and bring unmatched creativity to the industry, creating a unique level of customer experience and satisfaction.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job&nbsp;Position: Inventory Manager&nbsp;
Job Location: Lugbe, Apo, Lokogoma, Jabi, Gwarimpa, Kubwa, and Maitama - Abuja (FCT).&nbsp;

Key Responsibilities


	Oversee inventory operations across multiple store locations.
	Monitor stock levels and ensure inventory accuracy.
	Conduct regular stock counts, audits, and reconciliations.
	Investigate and resolve inventory discrepancies.
	Develop and implement inventory control procedures.
	Analyze inventory reports and provide recommendations.
	Coordinate stock transfers between branches.
	Work closely with purchasing, warehouse, and store teams to maintain optimal stock levels.
	Minimize stock losses, damages, and expired products.
	Train and supervise inventory personnel where necessary.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in a relevant field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in inventory management, preferably in retail or FMCG.
	Strong knowledge of inventory control systems and Microsoft Excel.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Strong leadership and communication skills.
	Ability to&nbsp;work under pressure and meet deadlines.


What We&#39;re Looking For:


	A proactive thinker who identifies problems before they become costly.
	Someone who can make data-driven decisions.
	A candidate with strong organizational and time management skills.
	A team player with excellent interpersonal skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: olivertwistvirtual@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Inventory Manager Job at Oliver Twist Supermarket




        
    

        
        Oliver Twist Supermarket is the brainchild of enigmatic directors along with a strong leadership and advisory board. The directors’ vast knowledge and experience in retail business have been deployed in the management and operation of Oliver Twist Supermarket since the inception of the first store in 2019 in Abuja, Nigeria. Our passion for the retail industry is not just to create financial success and growth but also to build a standard of excellence and bring unmatched creativity to the industry, creating a unique level of customer experience and satisfaction. 
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Inventory Manager 
Job Location: Lugbe, Apo, Lokogoma, Jabi, Gwarimpa, Kubwa, and Maitama - Abuja (FCT). 

Key Responsibilities


	Oversee inventory operations across multiple store locations.
	Monitor stock levels and ensure inventory accuracy.
	Conduct regular stock counts, audits, and reconciliations.
	Investigate and resolve inventory discrepancies.
	Develop and implement inventory control procedures.
	Analyze inventory reports and provide recommendations.
	Coordinate stock transfers between branches.
	Work closely with purchasing, warehouse, and store teams to maintain optimal stock levels.
	Minimize stock losses, damages, and expired products.
	Train and supervise inventory personnel where necessary.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in a relevant field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in inventory management, preferably in retail or FMCG.
	Strong knowledge of inventory control systems and Microsoft Excel.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Strong leadership and communication skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


What We're Looking For:


	A proactive thinker who identifies problems before they become costly.
	Someone who can make data-driven decisions.
	A candidate with strong organizational and time management skills.
	A team player with excellent interpersonal skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "ResumeSurge - We connect ambitious businesses with exceptional talent. We are a forward-thinking recruitment consultancy dedicated to helping companies find the right people to drive their success and helping skilled professionals find the right opportunities to advance their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Planning &amp; Budget Officer&nbsp;

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Facilities Management &amp; Administration (FM&amp;A)
Reports To: Head, Facilities Management &amp; Administration Division

Job Purpose


	The Planning &amp; Budget Officer is responsible for coordinating planning, budgeting, forecasting, performance monitoring, and reporting activities within the Facilities Management &amp; Administration (FM&amp;A) Division. The role ensures that departmental activities are aligned with approved budgets, corporate objectives, and partner requirements while facilitating cost control, budget optimization, contract monitoring, and efficient cost recovery.
	The position serves as a critical interface between technical departments, finance teams, partners, regulatory agencies, and vendors to support effective decision-making and operational excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Budgeting &amp; Financial Management:


	Coordinate the preparation of annual FM&amp;A operating and capital budgets.
	Develop and maintain five-year budget plans and long-term financial forecasts.
	Support Section Heads and Department Managers in budget development and management.
	Formulate and recommend budget defense strategies for management presentations and partner reviews.
	Monitor actual expenditures against approved budgets and identify variances.
	Analyze budget performance and recommend corrective actions where necessary.
	Track contract costs, service charges, and vendor spending to optimize costs.
	Prepare monthly budget performance reports and financial analyses.
	Ensure budget allocations remain aligned with business priorities and operational requirements.


Planning &amp; Performance Management:


	Coordinate integrated planning activities across the FM&amp;A Division.
	Develop and maintain planning schedules and ensure timely updates of projects and activities.
	Consolidate inputs from maintenance, administration, real estate, and project teams into a unified business plan.
	Support planning and scheduling activities using enterprise planning tools such as Primavera and MS Project.
	Prepare weekly and monthly key performance indicator (KPI) reports.
	Monitor departmental&nbsp;&nbsp;work programs and performance against established targets.
	Provide management with timely progress reports on strategic projects and initiatives.


Reporting &amp; Business Support:


	Prepare weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual performance reports.
	Develop management presentations, dashboards, and business review materials.
	Support preparation of divisional budget defense and performance review documentation.
	Assist in preparing presentations for management, partners, and governance committees.
	Translate technical activities and operational requirements into meaningful financial information for stakeholders.
	Provide data-driven insights to support management decision-making.


Stakeholder &amp; Contract Management:


	Liaise with finance teams, supply chain management, partners, and regulatory bodies on budget and planning matters.
	Coordinate with vendors and finance departments to facilitate timely invoice processing and payments.
	Monitor FM&amp;A contract status and ensure renewals are completed before expiry.
	Work with procurement and internal control teams to ensure compliance with delegated authority limits and procurement procedures.
	Support oversight of purchase orders, service requests, and payment processes.
	Foster strong working relationships with internal and external stakeholders to ensure efficient operations.


Compliance &amp; Governance:


	Ensure budget activities comply with company policies, financial procedures, and partner requirements.
	Monitor adherence to approved budgets and recommend budget realignments when necessary.
	Support audit reviews and provide required budget and planning documentation.
	Maintain accurate planning, budgeting, and contract records.
	Keep abreast of evolving financial processes, systems, and regulatory requirements.


Key Accountabilities:


	Timely preparation and submission of annual and long-term budgets.
	Effective monitoring and control of FM&amp;A expenditure.
	Accurate financial forecasting and budget performance reporting.
	Successful coordination of budget defense and cost recovery activities.
	Timely contract renewals without regularization issues.
	Reliable KPI reporting and performance tracking.
	Effective stakeholder engagement and relationship management.


HSE Responsibilities:


	Comply with all company health, safety, security, and environmental requirements.
	Participate in safety meetings, drills, and awareness programs.
	Report unsafe acts, incidents, and hazardous conditions promptly.
	Perform assigned duties in a safe and responsible manner.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of five (5) years of post-NYSC professional experience in planning, budgeting, financial analysis, project controls, or business performance management.


Technical Competencies:


	Budget Planning and Control
	Financial Analysis and Reporting
	Cost Monitoring and Forecasting
	Performance Management and KPI Reporting
	Contract Administration Support
	Project Planning and Scheduling
	Business Planning and Budget Defense
	Data Analysis and Interpretation
	Stakeholder Management
	Procurement Process Understanding.

Planning & Budget Officer Job at ResumeSurge




        
    

        
        ResumeSurge - We connect ambitious businesses with exceptional talent. We are a forward-thinking recruitment consultancy dedicated to helping companies find the right people to drive their success and helping skilled professionals find the right opportunities to advance their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Planning & Budget Officer 

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Facilities Management & Administration (FM&A)
Reports To: Head, Facilities Management & Administration Division

Job Purpose
     

            
    




	The Planning & Budget Officer is responsible for coordinating planning, budgeting, forecasting, performance monitoring, and reporting activities within the Facilities Management & Administration (FM&A) Division. The role ensures that departmental activities are aligned with approved budgets, corporate objectives, and partner requirements while facilitating cost control, budget optimization, contract monitoring, and efficient cost recovery.
	The position serves as a critical interface between technical departments, finance teams, partners, regulatory agencies, and vendors to support effective decision-making and operational excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Budgeting & Financial Management:


	Coordinate the preparation of annual FM&A operating and capital budgets.
	Develop and maintain five-year budget plans and long-term financial forecasts.
	Support Section Heads and Department Managers in budget development and management.
	Formulate and recommend budget defense strategies for management presentations and partner reviews.
	Monitor actual expenditures against approved budgets and identify variances.
	Analyze budget performance and recommend corrective actions where necessary.
	Track contract costs, service charges, and vendor spending to optimize costs.
	Prepare monthly budget performance reports and financial analyses.
	Ensure budget allocations remain aligned with business priorities and operational requirements.


Planning & Performance Management:


	Coordinate integrated planning activities across the FM&A Division.
	Develop and maintain planning schedules and ensure timely updates of projects and activities.
	Consolidate inputs from maintenance, administration, real estate, and project teams into a unified business plan.
	Support planning and scheduling activities using enterprise planning tools such as Primavera and MS Project.
	Prepare weekly and monthly key performance indicator (KPI) reports.
	Monitor departmental  work programs and performance against established targets.
	Provide management with timely progress reports on strategic projects and initiatives.


Reporting & Business Support:


	Prepare weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual performance reports.
	Develop management presentations, dashboards, and business review materials.
	Support preparation of divisional budget defense and performance review documentation.
	Assist in preparing presentations for management, partners, and governance committees.
	Translate technical activities and operational requirements into meaningful financial information for stakeholders.
	Provide data-driven insights to support management decision-making.


Stakeholder & Contract Management:


	Liaise with finance teams, supply chain management, partners, and regulatory bodies on budget and planning matters.
	Coordinate with vendors and finance departments to facilitate timely invoice processing and payments.
	Monitor FM&A contract status and ensure renewals are completed before expiry.
	Work with procurement and internal control teams to ensure compliance with delegated authority limits and procurement procedures.
	Support oversight of purchase orders, service requests, and payment processes.
	Foster strong working relationships with internal and external stakeholders to ensure efficient operations.


Compliance & Governance:


	Ensure budget activities comply with company policies, financial procedures, and partner requirements.
	Monitor adherence to approved budgets and recommend budget realignments when necessary.
	Support audit reviews and provide required budget and planning documentation.
	Maintain accurate planning, budgeting, and contract records.
	Keep abreast of evolving financial processes, systems, and regulatory requirements.


Key Accountabilities:


	Timely preparation and submission of annual and long-term budgets.
	Effective monitoring and control of FM&A expenditure.
	Accurate financial forecasting and budget performance reporting.
	Successful coordination of budget defense and cost recovery activities.
	Timely contract renewals without regularization issues.
	Reliable KPI reporting and performance tracking.
	Effective stakeholder engagement and relationship management.


HSE Responsibilities:


	Comply with all company health, safety, security, and environmental requirements.
	Participate in safety meetings, drills, and awareness programs.
	Report unsafe acts, incidents, and hazardous conditions promptly.
	Perform assigned duties in a safe and responsible manner.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of five (5) years of post-NYSC professional experience in planning, budgeting, financial analysis, project controls, or business performance management.


Technical Competencies:


	Budget Planning and Control
	Financial Analysis and Reporting
	Cost Monitoring and Forecasting
	Performance Management and KPI Reporting
	Contract Administration Support
	Project Planning and Scheduling
	Business Planning and Budget Defense
	Data Analysis and Interpretation
	Stakeholder Management
	Procurement Process Understanding.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Telecoms Maintenance Engineer",
  "description": "ResumeSurge - We connect ambitious businesses with exceptional talent. We are a forward-thinking recruitment consultancy dedicated to helping companies find the right people to drive their success &mdash; and helping skilled professionals find the right opportunities to advance their careers.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Telecoms Maintenance Engineer

Job Location: Port Harcourt,&nbsp; Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations / Information &amp; Telecommunications
Reports To: Telecommunications Manager

Job Purpose


	The Telecoms Maintenance Engineer is responsible for ensuring the reliability, availability, maintenance, upgrade, and continuous improvement of telecommunications systems across offshore and onshore production facilities.
	The role supports the design, installation, operation, troubleshooting, and lifecycle management of critical communication, surveillance, navigation, and networking systems while ensuring compliance with company standards and industry best practices.


Key Responsibilities
Telecommunications Operations &amp; Maintenance:


	Operate, inspect, maintain, and troubleshoot telecommunications systems and infrastructure.
	Ensure high availability and reliability of field telecommunications equipment and services.
	Perform routine assessments of telecom systems and recommend improvements where required.
	Investigate equipment failures, diagnose root causes, and implement corrective actions.
	Support preventive and corrective maintenance programs for critical telecommunications assets.


&nbsp;Engineering &amp; Technical Support:


	Carry out engineering studies for the design, installation, and standardization of telecommunications systems, masts, grounding systems, and related infrastructure.
	Develop technical specifications and scopes of work for telecom projects, upgrades, and modifications.
	Prepare engineering reports, technical recommendations, and project documentation.
	Review existing systems and recommend modernization, replacement, or integration initiatives where necessary.
	Update engineering drawings, technical manuals, and asset documentation.


Project Management:


	Support the execution of telecommunication projects from planning through commissioning.
	Evaluate technical bids and provide recommendations during contractor selection processes.
	Supervise installation, testing, commissioning, and handover of telecommunications systems.
	Participate in Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT), Site Acceptance Tests (SAT), and commissioning activities.
	Track project deliverables to ensure completion within approved schedules and budgets.


Contractor &amp; Vendor Management:


	Prepare technical dossiers and scopes of work for requests for quotation (RFQs).
	Monitor the performance of maintenance contractors and specialist service providers.
	Supervise third-party contractors during installation and maintenance activities.
	Participate in material inspections, technical evaluations, and supplier engagements.
	Provide technical support during procurement and contract administration processes.


Qualifications
Education:


	B.Sc. Degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Alternatively, an HND in a relevant engineering discipline with outstanding academic performance.


Experience:


	Minimum of 15 years of hands-on experience in enterprise data networking and operational project management environments.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in telecommunications or network engineering operations and maintenance.
	Experience supporting telecommunications systems in industrial, energy, offshore, or oil and gas environments is an advantage.


Technical Competencies:


	Telecommunications Engineering
	Enterprise Networking
	Fibre Optic Systems
	Microwave Communication Systems
	Radio Communication Technologies
	Telecommunications Infrastructure Design
	CCTV and Security Systems
	VSAT and Satellite Communications
	Project Management
	Contractor Supervision
	Technical Documentation
	Technical Bid Evaluation
	Maintenance Planning
	Asset Integrity Management
	Computer-Aided Design and Engineering Applications
	Microsoft Office Suite.


Behavioral Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and presentation skills.
	Effective stakeholder and contractor management.
	Ability to work independently and within cross-functional teams.
	Strong organizational and planning skills.
	High level of accountability and attention to detail.
	Continuous improvement mindset.


Systems Under Responsibility
The role covers operation and maintenance of:


	Data Networks
	Telephony Systems
	Microwave Communication Systems
	Fibre Optic Networks
	VSAT Communication Systems
	Public Address &amp; General Alarm (PA/GA) Systems
	CCTV Systems
	Video Management and Surveillance Systems
	Access Control Systems
	GMDSS Systems
	UHF/VHF Radio Systems
	Marine and Aeronautical Radio Systems
	RACON and Navigation Aid Systems
	Non-Directional Beacons (NDB)
	Radar and AIS Systems
	IPTV Systems
	Video Conferencing Systems
	Infrastructure Monitoring Systems
	Security and Safety Surveillance Systems.


Key Accountabilities:


	Ensure reliable and uninterrupted telecommunications services across all operational locations.
	Deliver telecommunications projects safely, on schedule, and within budget.
	Maintain compliance with telecommunications standards, installation rules, and company requirements.
	Ensure timely delivery of technical reports, specifications, and engineering documentation.
	Drive continuous improvement and optimization of telecommunications systems and contractor performance.


Working Conditions:


	Supports telecommunications operations across multiple onshore and offshore facilities, including production sites, FPSOs, and drilling rigs.
	Requires periodic field visits, inspections, project supervision, and commissioning activities.
	May be required to provide technical support during planned shutdowns, critical maintenance activities, and project implementations.

Telecoms Maintenance Engineer Job at ResumeSurge




        
    

        
        ResumeSurge - We connect ambitious businesses with exceptional talent. We are a forward-thinking recruitment consultancy dedicated to helping companies find the right people to drive their success — and helping skilled professionals find the right opportunities to advance their careers.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Telecoms Maintenance Engineer

Job Location: Port Harcourt,  Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Operations / Information & Telecommunications
Reports To: Telecommunications Manager

Job Purpose
     

            
    




	The Telecoms Maintenance Engineer is responsible for ensuring the reliability, availability, maintenance, upgrade, and continuous improvement of telecommunications systems across offshore and onshore production facilities.
	The role supports the design, installation, operation, troubleshooting, and lifecycle management of critical communication, surveillance, navigation, and networking systems while ensuring compliance with company standards and industry best practices.


Key Responsibilities
Telecommunications Operations & Maintenance:


	Operate, inspect, maintain, and troubleshoot telecommunications systems and infrastructure.
	Ensure high availability and reliability of field telecommunications equipment and services.
	Perform routine assessments of telecom systems and recommend improvements where required.
	Investigate equipment failures, diagnose root causes, and implement corrective actions.
	Support preventive and corrective maintenance programs for critical telecommunications assets.


 Engineering & Technical Support:


	Carry out engineering studies for the design, installation, and standardization of telecommunications systems, masts, grounding systems, and related infrastructure.
	Develop technical specifications and scopes of work for telecom projects, upgrades, and modifications.
	Prepare engineering reports, technical recommendations, and project documentation.
	Review existing systems and recommend modernization, replacement, or integration initiatives where necessary.
	Update engineering drawings, technical manuals, and asset documentation.


Project Management:


	Support the execution of telecommunication projects from planning through commissioning.
	Evaluate technical bids and provide recommendations during contractor selection processes.
	Supervise installation, testing, commissioning, and handover of telecommunications systems.
	Participate in Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT), Site Acceptance Tests (SAT), and commissioning activities.
	Track project deliverables to ensure completion within approved schedules and budgets.


Contractor & Vendor Management:


	Prepare technical dossiers and scopes of work for requests for quotation (RFQs).
	Monitor the performance of maintenance contractors and specialist service providers.
	Supervise third-party contractors during installation and maintenance activities.
	Participate in material inspections, technical evaluations, and supplier engagements.
	Provide technical support during procurement and contract administration processes.


Qualifications
Education:


	B.Sc. Degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Alternatively, an HND in a relevant engineering discipline with outstanding academic performance.


Experience:


	Minimum of 15 years of hands-on experience in enterprise data networking and operational project management environments.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in telecommunications or network engineering operations and maintenance.
	Experience supporting telecommunications systems in industrial, energy, offshore, or oil and gas environments is an advantage.


Technical Competencies:


	Telecommunications Engineering
	Enterprise Networking
	Fibre Optic Systems
	Microwave Communication Systems
	Radio Communication Technologies
	Telecommunications Infrastructure Design
	CCTV and Security Systems
	VSAT and Satellite Communications
	Project Management
	Contractor Supervision
	Technical Documentation
	Technical Bid Evaluation
	Maintenance Planning
	Asset Integrity Management
	Computer-Aided Design and Engineering Applications
	Microsoft Office Suite.


Behavioral Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and presentation skills.
	Effective stakeholder and contractor management.
	Ability to work independently and within cross-functional teams.
	Strong organizational and planning skills.
	High level of accountability and attention to detail.
	Continuous improvement mindset.


Systems Under Responsibility
The role covers operation and maintenance of:


	Data Networks
	Telephony Systems
	Microwave Communication Systems
	Fibre Optic Networks
	VSAT Communication Systems
	Public Address & General Alarm (PA/GA) Systems
	CCTV Systems
	Video Management and Surveillance Systems
	Access Control Systems
	GMDSS Systems
	UHF/VHF Radio Systems
	Marine and Aeronautical Radio Systems
	RACON and Navigation Aid Systems
	Non-Directional Beacons (NDB)
	Radar and AIS Systems
	IPTV Systems
	Video Conferencing Systems
	Infrastructure Monitoring Systems
	Security and Safety Surveillance Systems.


Key Accountabilities:


	Ensure reliable and uninterrupted telecommunications services across all operational locations.
	Deliver telecommunications projects safely, on schedule, and within budget.
	Maintain compliance with telecommunications standards, installation rules, and company requirements.
	Ensure timely delivery of technical reports, specifications, and engineering documentation.
	Drive continuous improvement and optimization of telecommunications systems and contractor performance.


Working Conditions:


	Supports telecommunications operations across multiple onshore and offshore facilities, including production sites, FPSOs, and drilling rigs.
	Requires periodic field visits, inspections, project supervision, and commissioning activities.
	May be required to provide technical support during planned shutdowns, critical maintenance activities, and project implementations.

title: Job Openings at Divine Mercy Specialist Hospital
content:
Divine Mercy Specialist Hospital, located in Ibele-Umuaka, Njaba LGA, Imo State, is a modern healthcare institution focused on delivering community health services. The facility provides both clinical and emergency care.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Senior Digital Content Strategist

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
